title: PHP-FPM Slow Request Counter Increases
app: Custom scripted input or HEC (PHP-FPM `pm.status_path` JSON)
spl:
index=php sourcetype="phpfpm:status"
| sort 0 _time
| delta slow_requests as slow_delta
| where slow_delta > 10
| table _time, host, pool, slow_requests, slow_delta
description: The `slow_requests` counter increments when requests exceed `request_slowlog_timeout`. Sustained increases pinpoint slow PHP code, database calls, or external APIs.
value: Surfaces PHP endpoints that breach slow thresholds so you can tune code or pool settings before queues explode.
implementation: Expose `pm.status_path` over loopback, poll JSON every minute, and parse `slow_requests`, `active processes`, and `idle processes`. Alert on delta spikes, not one-off counter values.
visualization: Line chart (`slow_requests` counter), bar chart (slow_delta per pool), correlation with upstream database latency.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom scripted input or HEC (PHP-FPM `pm.status_path`); Add On for Traefik Proxy patterns optional for edge logs.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=php` `sourcetype=phpfpm:status` JSON from `pm.status_path`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Poll `/status?json` on each pool; ensure `slow_requests` is extracted; use `delta` on the counter for alert logic.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=php sourcetype="phpfpm:status"
| sort 0 _time
| delta slow_requests as slow_delta
| where slow_delta > 10
| table _time, host, pool, slow_requests, slow_delta
```

Understanding this SPL

**PHP-FPM Slow Request Counter Increases** ‚Äî See the description and value fields in this use case JSON.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=php` `sourcetype=phpfpm:status` JSON from `pm.status_path`. **App/TA**: Custom scripted input or HEC (PHP-FPM `pm.status_path`); Add On for Traefik Proxy patterns optional for edge logs. Rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` to match your deployment.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scope to the documented index and sourcetype, then apply transforms, thresholds, and `timechart`/`stats` as in the SPL above.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm events exist, field extractions match your format, and thresholds reflect production baselines.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document ownership. Suggested visuals: Line chart (`slow_requests` counter), bar chart (slow_delta per pool), correlation with upstream database latency..
grandmaExplanation: We count how often requests are labeled slow so you can fix the worst pages before visitors wait forever.
